case_title: UNITED STATES OF AMERICA v. RONALD BRUCE MYERS
key_facts: Myers pleaded guilty to possessing an implement for counterfeiting state securities and transporting a stolen motor vehicle across state lines.
Myers still owes over $35,000 in restitution from the 2005 conviction.
Over $30,500 has been deposited in Myers’s trust account since 2013, mostly from family and friends.
Myers’s trust account contained $1,622 as of late 2022.
The government invoked the Mandatory Victims Restitution Act (MVRA) to turn over most of the remaining funds and apply them to Myers’s obligation.
legal_issues: Whether the Mandatory Victims Restitution Act (MVRA) applies to the gradual accumulation of cash deposits from family and friends in an inmate’s trust account.
decision: The panel majority held that § 3664(n) applies to substantial aggregated sums from multiple sources, such as family and friends, that gradually accrue in an inmate’s trust account. The district court properly invoked § 3664(n) to turn over deposits from family and friends that accrued to form a substantial sum in Myers’s inmate trust account.
reasoning_summary: The panel majority rejected Myers’s argument that § 3664(n) covers only one-time, lump-sum windfalls and sudden financial injections from a single source. Instead, they held that § 3664(n) applies to substantial aggregated sums from multiple sources, such as family and friends, that gradually accrue in an inmate’s trust account. The district court did not abuse its discretion in declining to hold an evidentiary hearing on the composition of Myers’s trust account.
